name: edwin-smith-surgical-papyrus
description: An Egyptian scroll detailed the examination and treatment of wounds, the oldest known surgical text.

name: ebers-papyrus-written
description: This Egyptian medical scroll recorded hundreds of remedies, ailments, and treatments for diseases.

name: asclepius-healing-cult
description: Greek healing temples devoted to Asclepius offered sleep-cures and treatments to the sick and injured.

name: hippocratic-oath-composed
description: Greek physicians adopted an ethical pledge defining a doctor's duties, still echoed in medicine today.

name: herophilus-dissects-human-body
description: In Alexandria, Herophilus performed systematic human dissections, founding the study of anatomy.

name: huangdi-neijing-compiled
description: China's foundational medical text outlined theories of the body, disease, and treatment by needling.

name: acupuncture-systematized
description: Chinese physicians formalized inserting fine needles at body points to treat illness and relieve pain.

name: celsus-de-medicina-encyclopedia
description: The Roman writer Celsus described surgery and the four signs of inflammation in a vast medical encyclopedia.

name: first-hospital-byzantine
description: Bishop Basil of Caesarea founded a charitable complex caring for the sick, a model for later hospitals.

name: hua-tuo-surgical-anesthesia
description: The Chinese surgeon Hua Tuo reputedly used a herbal anesthetic powder to perform abdominal operations.

name: al-razi-distinguishes-smallpox
description: The Persian physician Al-Razi wrote the first clear clinical account separating smallpox from measles.

name: al-zahrawi-surgical-encyclopedia
description: The Andalusian surgeon Al-Zahrawi illustrated hundreds of instruments in an influential surgical treatise.

name: constantine-african-translations
description: Constantine the African translated Arabic medical works into Latin, reviving European medical learning.

name: trotula-women-medicine
description: Texts attributed to Trotula of Salerno addressed women's health and childbirth in medieval Europe.

name: leprosy-hospitals-spread
description: Thousands of leprosaria were built across Europe to isolate and care for sufferers of leprosy.

name: first-medical-licensing-law
description: Emperor Frederick II required physicians in Sicily to be examined and licensed before practicing.

name: mondino-anatomy-dissection
description: Mondino de Luzzi wrote the first European anatomy manual based on hands-on human dissection in Bologna.

name: guy-de-chauliac-surgery-text
description: French surgeon Guy de Chauliac wrote a treatise that guided European surgery for two hundred years.

name: first-anatomy-theatre
description: The University of Padua built a permanent theatre where students watched public human dissections.

name: paracelsus-chemical-medicine
description: The Swiss physician Paracelsus pioneered using minerals and chemicals as remedies, challenging old dogma.

name: pare-improves-wound-care
description: The French surgeon Ambroise Pare replaced cauterizing wounds with gentler dressings and vessel ligation.

name: fracastoro-contagion-theory
description: Italian scholar Fracastoro proposed that tiny invisible seeds spread disease between people and objects.

name: first-pharmacopoeia
description: Nuremberg published a standardized list of medicines and recipes for apothecaries to follow.

name: santorio-metabolism-experiments
description: Santorio weighed himself for decades on a balance chair, founding the quantitative study of metabolism.

name: cinchona-bark-malaria
description: Jesuits brought Peruvian cinchona bark to Europe, the first effective remedy against malarial fevers.

name: wiseman-english-surgery
description: Richard Wiseman, called the father of English surgery, wrote influential works on wounds and treatment.

name: first-recorded-vital-statistics
description: John Graunt analyzed London's death records, founding epidemiology and the study of public health data.

name: smallpox-variolation-europe
description: Lady Mary Montagu introduced smallpox inoculation to Britain after observing the practice in Istanbul.

name: first-clinical-trial-scurvy
description: Naval surgeon James Lind tested treatments on sailors and found citrus fruit cured scurvy.

name: morgagni-founds-pathology
description: Giovanni Morgagni linked symptoms in life to organ damage found at autopsy, founding modern pathology.

name: first-maternity-hospital
description: London's lying-in hospitals opened to provide poor women with care and shelter during childbirth.

name: withering-digitalis
description: William Withering documented foxglove extract as a drug to treat dropsy and failing hearts.

name: pinel-reforms-asylums
description: Philippe Pinel unchained the mentally ill in Paris, promoting humane treatment over confinement.

name: first-dental-drill-foot
description: John Greenwood adapted a spinning-wheel pedal to power a dental drill, modernizing tooth treatment.

name: first-cesarean-mother-survives
description: Jesse Bennett reportedly performed an early cesarean section in America in which both mother and child lived.

name: homeopathy-founded
description: Samuel Hahnemann proposed treating illness with highly diluted substances, founding homeopathy.

name: first-blood-pressure-measurement
description: Stephen Hales first measured arterial blood pressure by inserting a tube into a horse's artery.

name: ophthalmoscope-invented
description: Hermann von Helmholtz built a device to look into the living eye, transforming the study of vision.

name: first-nursing-textbook
description: Florence Nightingale published Notes on Nursing, laying the foundations of professional patient care.

name: chloroform-eases-childbirth
description: James Simpson popularized chloroform anesthesia, and Queen Victoria used it to deliver a baby.

name: lister-spray-operating-theatre
description: Joseph Lister introduced a carbolic acid spray in the operating theatre to kill germs during surgery.

name: diphtheria-antitoxin-developed
description: Emil von Behring's antitoxin treated diphtheria, the first serum therapy and an early Nobel-winning advance.

name: ehrlich-magic-bullet
description: Paul Ehrlich's Salvarsan treated syphilis, launching chemotherapy and the search for magic-bullet drugs.

name: vitamins-discovered
description: Casimir Funk coined the word vitamine after finding nutrients whose lack caused diseases like beriberi.

name: first-typhoid-vaccine
description: Almroth Wright developed a vaccine against typhoid fever, later credited with saving wartime soldiers.

name: blood-groups-transfusion-safe
description: Matching donors by Landsteiner's blood groups made transfusions reliable for the first time.

name: bcg-tuberculosis-vaccine
description: The BCG vaccine against tuberculosis was first given to a child, becoming one of the world's most used.

name: iron-lung-developed
description: The iron lung used air pressure to breathe for paralyzed polio patients, keeping thousands alive.

name: blood-bank-established
description: A Chicago hospital opened the first blood bank, storing donated blood for transfusion on demand.

name: pap-smear-introduced
description: George Papanicolaou's cervical smear test let doctors catch cancer early, saving countless lives.

name: streptomycin-cures-tb
description: Selman Waksman's team isolated streptomycin, the first antibiotic effective against tuberculosis.

name: first-kidney-transplant
description: Surgeons in Boston transplanted a kidney between identical twins, the first lasting organ transplant.

name: birth-control-pill-developed
description: Researchers developed the first oral contraceptive, giving women reliable control over pregnancy.

name: first-hip-replacement
description: John Charnley pioneered a durable artificial hip joint, freeing arthritis sufferers from chronic pain.

name: first-liver-transplant
description: Thomas Starzl attempted the first human liver transplant, pioneering a now life-saving operation.

name: first-lung-transplant
description: Surgeon James Hardy performed the first human lung transplant, opening a new field of organ surgery.

name: ct-scanner-invented
description: Godfrey Hounsfield built the first CT scanner, letting doctors see cross-sections inside the body.

name: first-ultrasound-imaging
description: Ian Donald used sonar-like ultrasound to image the womb, making it routine to scan unborn babies.

name: monoclonal-antibodies-created
description: Kohler and Milstein produced identical antibodies in the lab, enabling new diagnostics and drugs.

name: first-statin-discovered
description: Akira Endo isolated the first statin, leading to drugs that lower cholesterol and prevent heart disease.

name: last-natural-smallpox-case
description: A hospital cook in Somalia became the last person to catch smallpox naturally, ending its spread.

name: first-hepatitis-b-vaccine
description: The first vaccine against hepatitis B was approved, later credited with preventing liver cancer.

name: azt-approved-aids
description: AZT became the first drug approved to treat HIV, slowing the virus in patients with AIDS.

name: human-genome-completed
description: Scientists finished sequencing the human genome, reading the full genetic blueprint of our species.

name: first-face-transplant
description: Surgeons in France performed the world's first partial face transplant on a disfigured patient.

name: induced-stem-cells-created
description: Shinya Yamanaka turned ordinary skin cells into stem cells, opening new paths in regenerative medicine.

name: hpv-vaccine-approved
description: The first vaccine against human papillomavirus was approved, aiming to prevent cervical cancer.

name: first-gene-therapy-approved-west
description: US regulators approved a therapy that reprograms a patient's own cells to attack their cancer.

name: first-malaria-vaccine-rollout
description: The WHO endorsed the first malaria vaccine for children, targeting a disease that kills hundreds of thousands.

name: first-pig-heart-transplant
description: Surgeons transplanted a genetically modified pig heart into a human, a milestone in xenotransplantation.

name: anatomy-act-passed
description: Britain's Anatomy Act gave schools legal access to corpses, ending the trade in stolen bodies.

name: first-modern-prosthetic-limb
description: Benjamin Palmer patented an artificial leg with bending joints, advancing prosthetics for amputees.

name: first-rabies-vaccine
description: Louis Pasteur saved a boy bitten by a rabid dog with the first vaccine against rabies.

name: first-organ-grown-lab
description: Surgeons implanted bladders grown from patients' own cells, an early triumph of tissue engineering.

name: keyhole-laparoscopic-surgery
description: A French surgeon removed a gallbladder through tiny incisions, launching minimally invasive surgery.

name: first-laser-eye-surgery
description: Surgeons first used a laser to reshape the cornea, leading to vision correction for millions.
